location_precision.why: Every one of the six findings carries a usable file:line (e.g. "src/sync.py:14", "src/report.py:11").

interaction_synthesis.why: The report.py:5 finding gestures that the handle leaks "on any exception raised in the loop" — which the :11 finding supplies (AttributeError/IndexError) — but never states this combination as a compound failure mode with its mechanism.

causal_clarity.why: Each finding states a failure mechanism (e.g. floor division "drops the final partial page," mutable default "persists across calls," f-string "enabling SQL injection"), not just a label.

interaction_synthesis.why: Each finding is described in isolation with no compound failure mode explained — e.g. the swallowed exception at :39 is never connected mechanistically to the persistent `seen` state at :24 or any other defect.

causal_clarity.why: Every finding states the failure mechanism and triggering conditions, e.g. mutable default `seen=[]` 'persists across calls, so users synced in one invocation are silently skipped in every later invocation.'
